name: Shvetashvatara Upanishad
summary: The Shvetashvatara Upanishad is unique in its theistic flavor, identifying Brahman with Rudra-Shiva. It teaches that the individual soul, the world, and God are distinct yet united, and that liberation comes through devotion and knowledge of the one God.

verses.sanskrit:
एको देवः सर्वभूतेषु गूढः सर्वव्यापी सर्वभूतान्तरात्मा |
कर्माध्यक्षः सर्वभूताधिवासः साक्षी चेता केवलो निर्गुणश्च ||६.११||
verses.transliteration:
eko devaḥ sarvabhūteṣu gūḍhaḥ sarvavyāpī sarvabhūtāntarātmā |
karmādhyakṣaḥ sarvabhūtādhivāsaḥ sākṣī cetā kevalo nirguṇaś ca ||6.11||
verses.english_translation: The one God, hidden in all beings, all-pervading, the inner Self of all beings, the overseer of all actions, dwelling in all beings, the witness, the pure consciousness, the one, without qualities.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: एकः देवः — अद्वितीयः परमेश्वरः — सर्वभूतेषु गूढः — सर्वेषु प्राणिषु अन्तर्हितः।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: The one God — without a second — is hidden in all beings as their inner Self. He is the witness of all actions, pure consciousness, and beyond all qualities. Knowing this one God, one is freed from all bonds.

verses.sanskrit:
द्वा सुपर्णा सयुजा सखाया समानं वृक्षं परिषस्वजाते |
तयोरन्यः पिप्पलं स्वाद्वत्त्यनश्नन्नन्यो अभिचाकशीति ||४.६||
verses.transliteration:
dvā suparṇā sayujā sakhāyā samānaṃ vṛkṣaṃ pariṣasvajāte |
tayor anyaḥ pippalaṃ svādv atty anaśnann anyo abhicākaśīti ||4.6||
verses.english_translation: Two birds, companions always united, cling to the same tree. One eats the sweet fruit; the other, not eating, simply watches.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: द्वौ सुपर्णौ — जीवेश्वरौ। जीवः कर्मफलानि भुङ्क्ते। ईश्वरः साक्षी।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: The two birds are the individual soul (jiva) and the Lord (Ishvara). The jiva eats the fruits of karma; the Lord is the witness. When the jiva recognizes its identity with the witnessing Lord, it is liberated.

verses.sanskrit:
भयादस्याग्निस्तपति भयात्तपति सूर्यः |
भयादिन्द्रश्च वायुश्च मृत्युर्धावति पञ्चमः ||६.३||
verses.transliteration:
bhayād asyāgnis tapati bhayāt tapati sūryaḥ |
bhayād indraś ca vāyuś ca mṛtyur dhāvati pañcamaḥ ||6.3||
verses.english_translation: Out of fear of Him, fire burns; out of fear of Him, the sun shines; out of fear of Him, Indra and Vayu, and death as the fifth, run.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: अस्य — परमेश्वरस्य — भयात् — अनुशासनात् — अग्निः तपति। सर्वे देवाः तस्य आज्ञां पालयन्ति।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: Out of reverence and fear of the supreme Lord, fire burns, the sun shines, and all the cosmic forces perform their functions. The entire universe operates under His governance.

verses.sanskrit:
एको देवः सर्वभूतेषु गूढः सर्वव्यापी सर्वभूतान्तरात्मा |
कर्माध्यक्षः सर्वभूताधिवासः साक्षी चेता केवलो निर्गुणश्च ||६.११||
verses.transliteration:
eko devaḥ sarvabhūteṣu gūḍhaḥ sarvavyāpī sarvabhūtāntarātmā |
karmādhyakṣaḥ sarvabhūtādhivāsaḥ sākṣī cetā kevalo nirguṇaś ca ||6.11||
verses.english_translation: The one God, hidden in all beings, all-pervading, the inner Self of all beings, the overseer of all actions, dwelling in all beings, the witness, the pure consciousness, the one, without qualities.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: एकः देवः — अद्वितीयः परमेश्वरः — सर्वभूतेषु गूढः।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: The one God is hidden in all beings as their inner Self. He is the witness of all actions, pure consciousness, and beyond all qualities. Knowing this one God, one is freed from all bonds.

verses.sanskrit:
एको रुद्रो न द्वितीयाय तस्थुर्य इमाँल्लोकानीशत ईशनीभिः |
प्रत्यङ्जनांस्तिष्ठति सञ्चुकोचान्तकाले संसृज्य विश्वा भुवनानि गोपाः ||३.२||
verses.transliteration:
eko rudro na dvitīyāya tasthur ya imāṃl lokān īśata īśanībhiḥ |
pratyañjanāṃs tiṣṭhati sañcukocāntakāle saṃsṛjya viśvā bhuvanāni gopāḥ ||3.2||
verses.english_translation: One Rudra — no second — rules these worlds with his powers. He stands within all people. Having created all worlds, he withdraws them at the end of time. He is the protector.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: एकः रुद्रः — अद्वितीयः। न द्वितीयाय — द्वितीयः नास्ति। इमान् लोकान् ईशते — सर्वान् लोकान् नियच्छति।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: There is only one Rudra — no second. He rules all worlds, stands within all beings, creates and dissolves all worlds. This is the theistic teaching of the Shvetashvatara — Brahman as the personal God Rudra-Shiva.

verses.sanskrit: युञ्जानः प्रथमं मनस्तत्त्वाय सविता धियः | अग्नेर्ज्योतिर्निचाय्य पृथिव्या अध्याभरत् ||२.१||
verses.transliteration: yuñjānaḥ prathamaṃ manas tattvāya savitā dhiyaḥ | agner jyotir nicāyya pṛthivyā adhy ābharat ||2.1||
verses.english_translation: First yoking the mind to the truth, Savita (the Sun) of the intellects, having perceived the light of fire, brought it from the earth.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: युञ्जानः — योगम् अभ्यसन् — प्रथमम् मनः — तत्त्वाय — ब्रह्मणि — युञ्जीत।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: The yoga practice begins with yoking the mind to the truth (Brahman). The Sun (Savita) represents the illuminating power of consciousness. The yogi first controls the mind, then perceives the inner light of the Self.

verses.sanskrit: यस्य देवे परा भक्तिर्यथा देवे तथा गुरौ | तस्यैते कथिता ह्यर्थाः प्रकाशन्ते महात्मनः ||६.२३||
verses.transliteration: yasya deve parā bhaktir yathā deve tathā gurau | tasyaite kathitā hy arthāḥ prakāśante mahātmanaḥ ||6.23||

word_meanings.meaning: as to God, so to the teacher
to that one these meanings shine forth

verses.english_translation: To one who has supreme devotion to God, and to the teacher as to God — to that great soul, these meanings shine forth.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: देवे परा भक्तिः — ईश्वरे उत्कृष्टा भक्तिः। गुरौ अपि तथैव। तस्य एते अर्थाः — उपनिषदर्थाः — प्रकाशन्ते।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: The teachings of the Upanishads are revealed only to one who has supreme devotion to both God and the teacher. Without this devotion, the words remain mere words. This verse emphasizes the importance of bhakti (devotion) alongside jnana (knowledge).

verses.sanskrit:
अणोरणीयान् महतो महीयानात्मा गुहायां निहितोऽस्य जन्तोः |
तमक्रतुः पश्यति वीतशोको धातुप्रसादान्महिमानमात्मनः ||३.२०||
verses.transliteration:
aṇor aṇīyān mahato mahīyān ātmā guhāyāṃ nihito'sya jantoḥ |
tam akratuḥ paśyati vītaśoko dhātuprasādān mahimānam ātmanaḥ ||3.20||

word_meanings.meaning: hidden in the cave of the heart

verses.english_translation: Smaller than the smallest, greater than the greatest, the Self is hidden in the heart of this creature. One free from desire and grief, by the grace of the Creator, sees the glory of the Self.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: अणोः अणीयान् — परमाणोरपि सूक्ष्मतरः। महतः महीयान् — महत्तत्त्वादपि महत्तरः। गुहायाम् — हृदयाकाशे — निहितः।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: The Self is subtler than the subtlest and vaster than the vastest. It is hidden in the cave of the heart. Only one who is free from desire and sorrow, by the grace of the Lord, can perceive the glory of the Self. This verse also appears in the Katha Upanishad.

verses.sanskrit: कालः स्वभावो नियतिर्यदृच्छा भूतानि योनिः पुरुष इति चिन्त्या | संयोग एषां न त्वात्मभावादात्माप्यनीशः सुखदुःखहेतोः ||१.२||
verses.transliteration: kālaḥ svabhāvo niyatir yadṛcchā bhūtāni yoniḥ puruṣa iti cintyā | saṃyoga eṣāṃ na tv ātmabhāvād ātmāpy anīśaḥ sukhaduḥkhahetoḥ ||1.2||

word_meanings.meaning: the Self is not the lord (of pleasure/pain)

verses.english_translation: Is it time, inherent nature, fate, chance, the elements, the womb, or the Person? — these are to be considered. It is not their combination, because the Self is not the lord of pleasure and pain.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: कालः — स्वभावः — नियतिः — यदृच्छा — भूतानि — योनिः — पुरुषः — एते सर्वे कारणत्वेन चिन्त्याः। किन्तु एषाम् संयोगः न — आत्मा अनीशः — सुखदुःखहेतोः।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: The Shvetashvatara opens by asking: what is the cause of the universe? Is it time, nature, fate, chance, elements, or the Self? The answer is that none of these alone, nor their combination, is the ultimate cause — because the Self is not subject to pleasure and pain. The true cause is the one God, Brahman.

verses.sanskrit: युञ्जानः प्रथमं मनस्तत्त्वाय सविता धियः | अग्नेर्ज्योतिर्निचाय्य पृथिव्या अध्याभरत् ||२.१||
verses.transliteration: yuñjānaḥ prathamaṃ manas tattvāya savitā dhiyaḥ | agner jyotir nicāyya pṛthivyā adhy ābharat ||2.1||
verses.english_translation: First yoking the mind to the truth, Savita of the intellects, having perceived the light of fire, brought it from the earth.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: युञ्जानः — योगम् अभ्यसन् — प्रथमम् मनः — तत्त्वाय — ब्रह्मणि — युञ्जीत।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: The yoga practice begins with yoking the mind to the truth (Brahman). The Sun (Savita) represents the illuminating power of consciousness. The yogi first controls the mind, then perceives the inner light of the Self.

verses.sanskrit: स्वदेहमरणिं कृत्वा प्रणवं चोत्तरारणिम् | ध्याननिर्मथनाभ्यासाद्देवं पश्येन्निगूढवत् ||१.१४||
verses.transliteration: svadeham araṇiṃ kṛtvā praṇavaṃ cottarāraṇim | dhyānanimathanābhyāsād devaṃ paśyen nigūḍhavat ||1.14||

word_meanings.meaning: making one's body the lower fire-stick
by the practice of churning through meditation

verses.english_translation: Making one's body the lower fire-stick and Om the upper fire-stick, by the practice of churning through meditation, one sees the God hidden within.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: स्वदेहम् अरणिम् — शरीरम् अधरारणिः। प्रणवम् उत्तरारणिम् — ओंकारः उत्तरारणिः। ध्याननिर्मथनेन — देवम् पश्येत्।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: The body is the lower fire-stick; Om is the upper fire-stick. By churning them together through meditation, the fire of knowledge is kindled and the God hidden within is revealed. This is the yoga of the Shvetashvatara — using the body and Om as instruments of meditation.

verses.sanskrit: एको हि रुद्रो न द्वितीयाय तस्थुर्य इमाँल्लोकानीशत ईशनीभिः ||३.२||
verses.transliteration: eko hi rudro na dvitīyāya tasthur ya imāṃl lokān īśata īśanībhiḥ ||3.2||
verses.english_translation: One Rudra — no second — rules these worlds with his powers.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: एकः रुद्रः — अद्वितीयः। न द्वितीयाय — द्वितीयः नास्ति। इमान् लोकान् ईशते — सर्वान् लोकान् नियच्छति।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: There is only one Rudra — no second. He rules all worlds with his powers. This is the theistic teaching of the Shvetashvatara — Brahman as the personal God Rudra-Shiva, the one without a second.

verses.sanskrit: सर्वाजीवे सर्वसंस्थे बृहन्ते तस्मिन्हंसो भ्राम्यते ब्रह्मचक्रे | पृथगात्मानं प्रेरितारं च मत्वा जुष्टस्ततस्तेनामृतत्वमेति ||१.६||
verses.transliteration: sarvājīve sarvasaṃsthe bṛhante tasmin haṃso bhrāmyate brahmacakre | pṛthag ātmānaṃ preritāraṃ ca matvā juṣṭas tatas tenāmṛtatvam eti ||1.6||

word_meanings.meaning: in which all life, all existence

verses.english_translation: In this wheel of Brahman, in which all life and all existence rests, the swan (individual soul) wanders. Knowing the Self as separate from the impeller, and being graced by it, one attains immortality.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: ब्रह्मचक्रे — संसारचक्रे — हंसः — जीवः — भ्राम्यते। प्रेरितारम् — ईश्वरम् — मत्वा — जुष्टः — अमृतत्वम् एति।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: The individual soul (hamsa) wanders in the wheel of samsara. When it recognizes the impeller — the Lord — and is graced by him, it attains immortality. This is the theistic path of the Shvetashvatara: grace of the Lord leads to liberation.

verses.sanskrit: त्वमग्निस्त्वं वायुस्त्वं सूर्यस्त्वं वासव | त्वं देवा सर्वे त्वं महाभूतानि | त्वं स्त्री त्वं पुमानसि त्वं कुमार उत वा कुमारी ||४.३||
verses.transliteration: tvam agnis tvaṃ vāyus tvaṃ sūryas tvaṃ vāsava | tvaṃ devā sarve tvaṃ mahābhūtāni | tvaṃ strī tvaṃ pumān asi tvaṃ kumāra uta vā kumārī ||4.3||

word_meanings.meaning: you are woman, you are man

verses.english_translation: You are fire, you are wind, you are the sun, you are Indra. You are all the gods; you are the great elements. You are woman, you are man, you are the youth, and also the maiden.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: त्वम् अग्निः — रुद्रः एव अग्निः। त्वम् वायुः — रुद्रः एव वायुः। सर्वम् रुद्रः एव।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: This beautiful verse declares that Rudra (Brahman) is everything — fire, wind, sun, all gods, all elements, man, woman, youth, and maiden. Brahman pervades all forms and is the inner reality of all existence. This is the vision of unity in diversity.

verses.sanskrit: नीलोऽथ पीत इति स्रुजत्येष रूपाणि बहूनि | अस्य माया महेश्वरस्य जगद्बभूव ||४.१||
verses.transliteration: nīlo'tha pīta iti sṛjaty eṣa rūpāṇi bahūni | asya māyā maheśvarasya jagad babhūva ||4.1||

word_meanings.meaning: the maya of the great Lord

verses.english_translation: He creates many forms — blue, yellow, and others. By the maya of the great Lord, the world came into being.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: नीलः पीतः — बहूनि रूपाणि — सृजति। माया — महेश्वरस्य — शक्तिः — जगत् बभूव।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: The great Lord creates the world of many colors and forms through his maya (creative power). The world is not an illusion in the sense of non-existence — it is real as an appearance, but its ultimate reality is Brahman. Maya is the Lord's creative power.

verses.sanskrit: तमीश्वराणां परमं महेश्वरं तं देवतानां परमं च दैवतम् | पतिं पतीनां परमं परस्ताद्विदाम देवं भुवनेशमीड्यम् ||६.७||
verses.transliteration: tam īśvarāṇāṃ paramaṃ maheśvaraṃ taṃ devatānāṃ paramaṃ ca daivatam | patiṃ patīnāṃ paramaṃ parastād vidāma devaṃ bhuvanešam īḍyam ||6.7||

word_meanings.meaning: the lord of the worlds, worthy of praise

verses.english_translation: Let us know that supreme great Lord of lords, that supreme deity of deities, that lord of lords, the God who is the lord of the worlds and worthy of praise.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: ईश्वराणाम् परमम् — सर्वेश्वराणाम् ईश्वरः। देवतानाम् परमम् — सर्वदेवानाम् देवः। भुवनेशम् — सर्वलोकेश्वरम्।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: The supreme Lord is the lord of all lords, the deity of all deities, the lord of all worlds. He is worthy of the highest praise and devotion. Knowing this supreme Lord, one attains liberation. This is the culminating teaching of the Shvetashvatara.

verses.sanskrit: इन्द्रो मायाभिः पुरुरूप ईयते | युक्ता ह्यस्य हरयः शता दश | अयं च हस्त्यायुधश्च | स एष एकः ||४.१४||
verses.transliteration: indro māyābhiḥ pururūpa īyate | yuktā hy asya harayaḥ śatā daśa | ayaṃ ca hasty āyudhaś ca | sa eṣa ekaḥ ||4.14||

word_meanings.meaning: Indra by his powers of maya

verses.english_translation: Indra appears in many forms through his powers of maya. His horses are a thousand. He is indeed one.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: इन्द्रः — परमेश्वरः — मायाभिः — शक्तिभिः — पुरुरूपः — बहुरूपः — ईयते। स एकः — अद्वितीयः।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: The Lord appears in many forms through his maya (creative power). Though appearing as many, he is indeed one. This is the teaching of unity in diversity — the one Brahman appears as the many through maya.

verses.sanskrit: न तस्य कार्यं करणं च विद्यते न तत्समश्चाभ्यधिकश्च दृश्यते | परास्य शक्तिर्विविधैव श्रूयते स्वाभाविकी ज्ञानबलक्रिया च ||६.८||
verses.transliteration: na tasya kāryaṃ karaṇaṃ ca vidyate na tat samaś cābhyadhikaś ca dṛśyate | parāsya śaktir vividhaiva śrūyate svābhāvikī jñānabalakriyā ca ||6.8||

word_meanings.meaning: none equal to him, none superior

verses.english_translation: He has no effect and no instrument. None is seen equal to him or superior. His supreme power is heard to be manifold — natural knowledge, strength, and action.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: न कार्यम् — न करणम् — ईश्वरस्य। न तत्समः — न अभ्यधिकः — कश्चित्। परा शक्तिः — स्वाभाविकी — ज्ञानबलक्रिया।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: The Lord has no external instrument or effect — he creates through his own inherent power. None is equal to him or superior. His power is natural — not acquired — and manifests as knowledge, strength, and action. This describes the absolute supremacy of Brahman.

verses.sanskrit: सर्वाजीवे सर्वसंस्थे बृहन्ते तस्मिन्हंसो भ्राम्यते ब्रह्मचक्रे ||१.६||
verses.transliteration: sarvājīve sarvasaṃsthe bṛhante tasmin haṃso bhrāmyate brahmacakre ||1.6||

word_meanings.meaning: in which all life, all existence

verses.english_translation: In this vast wheel of Brahman, in which all life and all existence rests, the swan (individual soul) wanders.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: ब्रह्मचक्रे — संसारचक्रे — हंसः — जीवः — भ्राम्यते — जन्ममरणचक्रे।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: The individual soul (hamsa/swan) wanders in the vast wheel of Brahman — the cycle of samsara. It wanders because it does not recognize its identity with the Lord. When it does recognize this, it is freed from the wheel.

verses.transliteration: pṛthag ātmānaṃ preritāraṃ ca matvā juṣṭas tatas tenāmṛtatvam eti ||1.6||
verses.english_translation: Having known the Self as separate from the impeller, and being graced by it, one attains immortality.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: पृथक् आत्मानम् — जीवम् — प्रेरितारम् — ईश्वरम् — मत्वा — जुष्टः — अमृतत्वम् एति।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: When the individual soul recognizes the Lord as the impeller and is graced by him, it attains immortality. This is the theistic path of the Shvetashvatara — grace of the Lord leads to liberation.

verses.sanskrit: ज्ञाज्ञौ द्वावजावीशनीशौ छायातपौ ब्रह्मपरौ च तौ | एकः सर्वभूतेषु गूढः ||१.९||
verses.transliteration: jñājñau dvāv ajāv īśanīśau chāyātapau brahmaparau ca tau | ekaḥ sarvabhūteṣu gūḍhaḥ ||1.9||

word_meanings.meaning: the knower and the non-knower, two

verses.english_translation: Two unborn ones — the knower and the non-knower, the lord and the non-lord, shadow and sunshine — and one hidden in all beings.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: ज्ञः — ईश्वरः — अज्ञः — जीवः — द्वौ अजौ। एकः — परमात्मा — सर्वभूतेषु गूढः।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: Two unborn ones — the Lord (the knower, the controller) and the individual soul (the non-knower, the controlled). And one — the supreme Self — hidden in all beings. This describes the three realities of the Shvetashvatara: God, soul, and world, with the supreme Self as the ground of all.

verses.sanskrit: यस्य देवे परा भक्तिर्यथा देवे तथा गुरौ | तस्यैते कथिता ह्यर्थाः प्रकाशन्ते महात्मनः ||६.२३||
verses.transliteration: yasya deve parā bhaktir yathā deve tathā gurau | tasyaite kathitā hy arthāḥ prakāśante mahātmanaḥ ||6.23||

word_meanings.meaning: as to God, so to the teacher
to that one these meanings shine forth

verses.english_translation: To one who has supreme devotion to God, and to the teacher as to God — to that great soul, these meanings shine forth.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: देवे परा भक्तिः — ईश्वरे उत्कृष्टा भक्तिः। गुरौ अपि तथैव। तस्य एते अर्थाः — उपनिषदर्थाः — प्रकाशन्ते।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: The teachings of the Upanishads are revealed only to one who has supreme devotion to both God and the teacher. Without this devotion, the words remain mere words. This concluding verse of the Shvetashvatara emphasizes that bhakti (devotion) is essential for the knowledge of Brahman to shine forth.

verses.sanskrit: यो देवोऽग्नौ योऽप्सु यो विश्वं भुवनमाविवेश | य ओषधीषु यो वनस्पतिषु तस्मै देवाय नमो नमः ||२.१७||
verses.transliteration: yo devo'gnau yo'psu yo viśvaṃ bhuvanam āviveśa | ya oṣadhīṣu yo vanaspatīṣu tasmai devāya namo namaḥ ||2.17||

word_meanings.meaning: the God who is in fire
who has entered the whole world

verses.english_translation: Salutation again and again to that God who is in fire, who is in water, who has entered the whole world, who is in plants and trees.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: यः देवः — अग्नौ — अप्सु — ओषधीषु — वनस्पतिषु — विश्वम् भुवनम् आविवेश — तस्मै नमः।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: This verse of salutation recognizes Brahman (Rudra) as present in all elements — fire, water, plants, trees, and the entire world. Salutation to that all-pervading God. This is the devotional expression of the Shvetashvatara's teaching of divine immanence.

verses.sanskrit: यो ब्रह्माणं विदधाति पूर्वं यो वै वेदांश्च प्रहिणोति तस्मै | तं ह देवमात्मबुद्धिप्रकाशं मुमुक्षुर्वै शरणमहं प्रपद्ये ||६.१८||
verses.transliteration: yo brahmāṇaṃ vidadhāti pūrvaṃ yo vai vedāṃś ca prahiṇoti tasmai | taṃ ha devam ātmabuddhiprakāśaṃ mumukṣur vai śaraṇam ahaṃ prapadye ||6.18||

word_meanings.meaning: who illumines the intellect of the Self
I, the seeker of liberation, take refuge

verses.english_translation: Who creates Brahma in the beginning and sends forth the Vedas to him — to that God who illumines the intellect of the Self, I, the seeker of liberation, take refuge.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: यः ब्रह्माणम् विदधाति — सृष्टेः आदौ ब्रह्माणम् उत्पादयति। वेदान् प्रहिणोति — ब्रह्मणे वेदान् ददाति। तम् — शरणम् प्रपद्ये।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: The Lord creates Brahma (the creator) at the beginning of each cosmic cycle and transmits the Vedas to him. He illumines the intellect of all seekers. The seeker of liberation takes refuge in this Lord — this is the devotional path of the Shvetashvatara.

verses.sanskrit: एको देवः सर्वभूतेषु गूढः सर्वव्यापी सर्वभूतान्तरात्मा | कर्माध्यक्षः सर्वभूताधिवासः साक्षी चेता केवलो निर्गुणश्च ||६.११||
verses.transliteration: eko devaḥ sarvabhūteṣu gūḍhaḥ sarvavyāpī sarvabhūtāntarātmā | karmādhyakṣaḥ sarvabhūtādhivāsaḥ sākṣī cetā kevalo nirguṇaś ca ||6.11||
verses.english_translation: The one God, hidden in all beings, all-pervading, the inner Self of all beings, the overseer of all actions, the witness, pure consciousness, the one, without qualities.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: एकः देवः — अद्वितीयः। सर्वभूतेषु गूढः — सर्वेषु प्राणिषु अन्तर्हितः। साक्षी — केवलः — निर्गुणः।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: The one God is hidden in all beings as their inner Self. He is the witness of all actions, pure consciousness, and beyond all qualities. Knowing this one God, one is freed from all bonds. This is the central teaching of the Shvetashvatara.

verses.sanskrit: नित्यो नित्यानां चेतनश्चेतनानामेको बहूनां यो विदधाति कामान् | तत्कारणं सांख्ययोगाधिगम्यं ज्ञात्वा देवं मुच्यते सर्वपाशैः ||६.१३||
verses.transliteration: nityo nityānāṃ cetanaś cetanānām eko bahūnāṃ yo vidadhāti kāmān | tat kāraṇaṃ sāṃkhyayogādhigamyaṃ jñātvā devaṃ mucyate sarvapāśaiḥ ||6.13||
verses.english_translation: The eternal among eternals, the conscious among the conscious, the one among the many who fulfills desires — knowing that God, attainable through Samkhya and Yoga, one is freed from all bonds.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: नित्यः नित्यानाम् — नित्यानाम् मध्ये नित्यः। चेतनः चेतनानाम् — चेतनानाम् मध्ये चेतनः। एकः बहूनाम् — बहूनाम् जीवानाम् एकः ईश्वरः।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: The Lord is the eternal among all eternals, the conscious among all conscious beings, the one who fulfills the desires of the many. Knowing this God through Samkhya (knowledge) and Yoga (meditation), one is freed from all bonds. This is the most celebrated verse of the Shvetashvatara.

verses.sanskrit: न तस्य कार्यं करणं च विद्यते न तत्समश्चाभ्यधिकश्च दृश्यते | परास्य शक्तिर्विविधैव श्रूयते स्वाभाविकी ज्ञानबलक्रिया च ||६.८||
verses.transliteration: na tasya kāryaṃ karaṇaṃ ca vidyate na tat samaś cābhyadhikaś ca dṛśyate | parāsya śaktir vividhaiva śrūyate svābhāvikī jñānabalakriyā ca ||6.8||
verses.english_translation: He has no effect and no instrument. None is seen equal to him or superior. His supreme power is manifold — natural knowledge, strength, and action.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: न कार्यम् — न करणम् — ईश्वरस्य। परा शक्तिः — स्वाभाविकी — ज्ञानबलक्रिया।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: The Lord has no external instrument — he creates through his own inherent power. None is equal to him or superior. His power is natural — not acquired — and manifests as knowledge, strength, and action. This describes the absolute supremacy of Brahman.

verses.sanskrit: सहस्रशीर्षा पुरुषः सहस्राक्षः सहस्रपात् | स भूमिं विश्वतो वृत्वाऽत्यतिष्ठद्दशाङ्गुलम् ||३.१४||
verses.transliteration: sahasraśīrṣā puruṣaḥ sahasrākṣaḥ sahasrapāt | sa bhūmiṃ viśvato vṛtvā'tyatiṣṭhad daśāṅgulam ||3.14||

word_meanings.meaning: the Person with a thousand heads
having covered the earth on all sides

verses.english_translation: The Person has a thousand heads, a thousand eyes, a thousand feet. Having covered the earth on all sides, he transcends it by ten fingers.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: सहस्रशीर्षा — अनन्तशीर्षः। सहस्राक्षः — अनन्तचक्षुः। भूमिम् वृत्वा — सर्वम् आवृत्य — दशाङ्गुलम् अत्यतिष्ठत् — अतिक्रान्तः।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: The cosmic Person (Purusha) has infinite heads, eyes, and feet — meaning he pervades all space. Having covered the entire earth, he transcends it by ten fingers — meaning he is both immanent (in the world) and transcendent (beyond it). This verse from the Purusha Sukta is quoted here.

verses.sanskrit: पुरुष एवेदं सर्वं यद्भूतं यच्च भव्यम् | उतामृतत्वस्येशानो यदन्नेनातिरोहति ||३.१५||
verses.transliteration: puruṣa evedaṃ sarvaṃ yad bhūtaṃ yac ca bhavyam | utāmṛtatvasyeśāno yad annenātirohati ||3.15||

word_meanings.meaning: the Person alone is all this
what has been and what will be

verses.english_translation: The Person alone is all this — what has been and what will be. He is the lord of immortality, who grows beyond food.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: पुरुषः एव — सर्वम् — भूतम् भव्यम् च। अमृतत्वस्य ईशानः — मोक्षस्य ईश्वरः।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: The Person (Brahman) alone is all this — past, present, and future. He is the lord of immortality — the one who grants liberation. He transcends food (the material world) — he is beyond all material limitation.

verses.sanskrit: एतज्ज्ञेयं नित्यमेवात्मसंस्थं नातः परं वेदितव्यं हि किञ्चित् | भोक्ता भोग्यं प्रेरितारं च मत्वा सर्वं प्रोक्तं त्रिविधं ब्रह्ममेतत् ||१.१२||
verses.transliteration: etaj jñeyaṃ nityam evātmasaṃsthaṃ nātaḥ paraṃ veditavyaṃ hi kiñcit | bhoktā bhogyaṃ preritāraṃ ca matvā sarvaṃ proktaṃ trividhaṃ brahma etat ||1.12||

word_meanings.meaning: this is to be known, eternal, established in the Self
nothing beyond this to be known
the enjoyer, the enjoyed, the impeller

verses.english_translation: This is to be known — eternal, established in the Self. There is nothing beyond this to be known. The enjoyer, the enjoyed, and the impeller — all this is declared as the threefold Brahman.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: एतत् ज्ञेयम् — आत्मसंस्थम् — नित्यम्। भोक्ता — जीवः। भोग्यम् — जगत्। प्रेरितारम् — ईश्वरः। त्रिविधम् ब्रह्म।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: The three realities of the Shvetashvatara are: the enjoyer (jiva/individual soul), the enjoyed (jagat/world), and the impeller (Ishvara/God). All three are Brahman in different aspects. Knowing this threefold Brahman, there is nothing more to be known.
